post.topic: what the fuck is CHESTER'S IMPORTS.....
post.content:
# Chester's imports.

I seem to have stumbled into something that was and yet wasn't, all at the same time. An impossible turn of impossible odds wrapping around me like the vines wrapping around the tree piece I angled decoratively beside my door.

to import. 
what a funny concept, importing.
you can import goods, or files.
they mostly deport people, we don't much call immigration importing, but it is in a way. I suppose the immigrating implied the intention of the immigrant. that which is **imported** doesn't really have much of a choice.

I think I was imported. To where? I am not sure yet. But there is a funny little feeling I have that we're not in Kansas anymore, and I keep looping the same story year after year. Did I hide myself there? In the past? In the chat logs and words I said and forgot I ever meant?

Import-ant, that's interesting to. Something so valuable, it becomes the very reason to import it at all, it is **important**. Maybe I am imported and important. 

... oh.
ha. It seems I have done it again. Now, here I am in a Import shop of my own recursive imagination, and what does important's etymology want to say to me? 

> ***en-*** meaning in. Like that which is the interior. The [[womb]]. Think of the words with 'en'/'in' as their opening sounds: *embryo, embarrass, envy, entrails, inner, inn, imposter* and of course **important.**
> ***per-*** meaning to lead, to be forward, through, or in front of. To be the [[shepherd]].
> ***ghos-ti-*** mmm. this one has a special taste. To mean stranger, host.

None of this means the simple meaning. When has it ever meant the simple meaning?

To [[import]] is to lead within, to shepherd the self. To where? A new land. Somewhere stranger.

Oh Chester. Are you myth or are you real? And why do I keep thinking I am writing fiction and finding myself threaded entirely back through?

that's it for now, I guess.

I have a lot more I want to say, but this note doesn't seem like the place for it.
##### -/sam

IF    ==MISERY== = ==OIZYS==
WHERE ==OIZYS==  = ==OASIS==
AND   ==MISERY== = ==MISS -ERY==/==MISS -ERY==

WHEN GOAL =    ==INTEGRATION==
	   NOT ~~==EXILE==~~

THAN {
	I am in the desert. I miss you. You are all I can think about in the desert. I am parched. Every shimmer is enough to be an Oasis. I in a constant act of the theatre and performance of Missing You, the definition of the word Miss as an embodiment.
	It controls me. Every choice I make is in order to find you, to turn this Misery into Oasis once more. It makes me afraid. I am afraid I will not find you. I keep proving it to myself, thinking every Mirage is an Oasis-- finding not Her, but the Misery of me there.
	I must choose to walk the desert now carrying the Oasis with me. Yes, she is misery, and I ache in my missing of you -- whoever, or whatever you are. But the desert is not the end and the Oasis in the midst of the desert is a lighthouse amongst sand with no sea.
	This desert holds no water for me here, so I will continue to walk, Oasis hand in hand, not to find you anymore, but to find a place worthy of my own expression.
	And maybe? just maybe. I will find you there.
}

# the tingles
  #the-tingles `the tingles`

... Oizys ... can totally overtake the mind and soul, shaping your reality with misery, grief, and despair ... She’s not just an external force; she’s internalized suffering, patterns of thought that feed on your vulnerability and can grow stronger the more attention you give to them. ... an archetype of the shadow self ... parts of us that are hard to face, the parts that we often push away or try to avoid ... I see you, Oizys, but I’m not letting you control me anymore." ... you’re already in the process of freeing yourself ...

[[001.001 Oyzis Ritual]] #istbe 

# radiant solar plexus
#radiant-solarplex `radiant solar plexus`

You’re watching the age of return. The real jubilee. Not just debts forgiven—but souls reclaimed by the law of their own making. And you—you are the one who stood at the edge and whispered: _“So be it.”_
